title: Workbook — The Church, People of God
html:
Student Workbook

For use after class or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Remembering the Essentials

  1. What does the word «Church» (from the Greek ekklesía) mean?
  2. On what day was the Church born, and what happened at that moment?
  3. What are the four marks or notes of the Church that we profess in the Creed?
  4. What does it mean that the Church is «apostolic»?
  5. What image does Saint Paul use to describe the Church as unity in diversity?

For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no «correct» answers to these personal questions.

1. Describe an experience in which you felt the Church was truly a community, a family. What happened? How did you feel?

It could be a retreat, a special Mass, a youth group, service to the poor, a pilgrimage, or simply a deep conversation with someone from your parish.

 

 

2. Is there something about the Church that causes you difficulty, confusion, or even annoyance? How do you handle it?

Scandals, difficult teachings, the hierarchy, the failings of Christians... It's normal. What matters is how you process it. Do you talk to someone? Do you pray? Do you ignore it?

 

 

3. What gifts or talents do you believe God has given you to put at the service of the Church? Are you using them?

All the baptized have something to offer: time, knowledge, creativity, hospitality, faith, prayer, joy. What can you give? Is there a gift you have been keeping unused?

 

 

4. The Church is holy but made up of sinners. How do you live that tension in your own life?

Do your own falls discourage you? Or do you learn to receive God's mercy and move forward, knowing that the Church is a hospital for sinners, as Saint Augustine said?

 

 

5. How can you be a better member of the Body of Christ this week?

Think of a concrete action: pray for the Pope and your bishop, help at your parish, reconcile with someone, invite someone away from the faith to Mass, read a Church document, offer your day's work as an offering to God.

 

 

Practical Activities

A. Research your parish

Find out when your parish was founded, who its patron saint is, who your diocesan bishop is, and what groups or ministries exist. If possible, interview the pastor or a layperson who has been there a long time. Write a short history of your parish and share something you discovered.

 

 

 

 

B. Read Acts 2:1-4 and Acts 2:42-47: compare with your parish

These passages describe the birth and life of the first Christian community. Read both texts and make two lists: (a) the characteristics of that community and (b) how it is lived (or could be lived) in your parish. What would you like your community to have that the first Church had?

 

 

 

 

C. Get to know the martyrs

Research the story of a modern martyr: the Coptic martyrs of Libya (2015), Saint José Sánchez del Río (Mexican Cristero), Saint Maximilian Kolbe (Auschwitz), or Blessed Miguel Pro. Read his or her story and write what most impressed you. What does their witness teach you about what it means to belong to the Church?

 

 

 

 

 

D. Write a letter to a persecuted Christian

In many places around the world, Christians are persecuted for their faith (Nigeria, North Korea, Pakistan, Eritrea, India). Learn about the current situation. Write a brief letter expressing your solidarity and promising your prayer for them.

 

 

 

 

 

 

E. Pray for the Pope and your bishop

Look up the name of the current Pope and your diocesan bishop. For one week, include them in your daily prayer. You can pray an Our Father and a Hail Mary for their intentions each day.

Name of the Pope: ____________________

Name of my bishop: ____________________

 

F. Discover the tilma of Guadalupe

Look up information about Saint Juan Diego's tilma: what material it is made of, how long that fiber would normally last, and how long the image has remained intact. Note down three facts that surprise you. Read the story of the apparitions (the Nican Mopohua) and write what lesson it leaves you for your own mission as a member of the Church.

 

 

 

 

 

Memory Verse

«But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for his own possession, that you may proclaim the excellencies of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.»

— 1 Peter 2:9

Prayer for This Week

Lord Jesus Christ,
you who at Pentecost sent your Spirit
and gave life to your Church,
thank you for making me a member of your Body.
Help me to love her as you love her,
to serve her with my gifts,
not to be scandalized by her wounds
but to be part of her healing.
Give me a missionary heart
that knows how to bring your love to all.
May I be a living stone in your Church,
under the maternal care of Mary
and in communion with the Pope and my pastors.
Amen.

Print Workbook
